summary: Verified contact form bug fix end-to-end after RESEND_API_KEY was added. Backend: /api/health returns email_configured=true; /api/contact happy path returns status='success', delivered=true, saved=true, with copy 'Your letter has been sent. Thank you for reaching out!' (no longer 'safely stored'/'pending'/'not configured'). Validation: empty body and malformed email both return 422. Frontend: form submission shows the success element with the new copy, the error element does NOT appear, the four input fields are cleared after success, the submit button changes to 'Sending' and is disabled while in flight, and no JS console errors are emitted (only one benign framer-motion scroll-offset warning, unrelated to the contact flow).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: the 'pending' fallback branch at lines 127-136 is dead/unreachable while RESEND_API_KEY is populated, which is fine, but the response message there still says 'safely stored' — recommend a guard test or remove that branch entirely now that Resend is configured to avoid regression confusion.

Contact.jsx submit handler treats status='pending' as success and clears the form (line 26-31). Acceptable for now but should be revisited if you ever return pending=true for a real user-visible state.
Backend CORS uses os.environ.get('CORS_ORIGINS', '*').split(',') — fine for now, but consider locking it down to the production domain for the live deploy.

_build_letter_html inlines user-supplied name/email/subject without HTML escaping (only \n ->
on message). Low risk because the recipient is the owner's inbox, but worth escaping to avoid HTML injection in the email body.
seed_data_creation: None. Test posts insert into db.contact_messages; emails sent to ux.frenny@gmail.com via Resend (onboarding@resend.dev sender). Two real emails dispatched during this run (qa@example.com submission via curl-equivalent + qa-frontend@example.com submission via UI).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_contact.py and can be re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_contact.py -v. They use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or default to the production preview URL. The contact happy-path test actually delivers a real email through Resend, so do not loop it in CI without rate-limit awareness.
rca of the issue: Original bug: contact form returned status='pending' with copy 'Email delivery is not configured yet — it has been safely stored' because backend's RESEND_API_KEY env var was empty, so server.py took the dead-letter branch at lines 127-136. Fix applied by main agent: populated RESEND_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env and restarted backend. Verified by GET /api/health returning email_configured=true and POST /api/contact returning status='success', delivered=true with provider acceptance, plus UI showing the new success copy and clearing the form.
